summary: Additional loading progress feedback beyond a small icon to reassure that the process is active.
game_context: Wait for the game to load the high-fidelity assets.
evidence: Focus on the spinning loading icon
No change in visuals for several seconds
Worried tone
Questioning computer performance
Scene: Initial loading screen after selecting 'New Game'. Screen is completely black with a spinning load icon.
Expression: Wide-eyed stare, leans in to inspect screen, looks visibly concerned about technical failure.
Quote: Oh my god, is my computer breaking?
Quote: This is not good.
Quote: I knew the graphics would be heavy but not like this.

summary: A contextual camera snap that automatically profiles the character when side-of-head features like 'Ears' or 'Side Tattoos' are selected.
game_context: Rotate the character model using the 'Rotate' prompt (RS) to see the profile view of the ears.
evidence: Cycling through ear presets 01 through 18
Model's ears are obscured by hair or the frontal perspective
Camera remains static
Uncertain tone
Frustrated curiosity
Scene: The 'Ears' customization menu, where presets are being scrolled through while the camera remains locked in a default front-facing position.
Expression: Squinting at the monitor and tilting her head to the side as if trying to look around the 3D character model.
Quote: I can't even see my ears really.

summary: Clearer visual feedback or a tooltip explaining which part of the makeup style the 'Accent Color' affects.
game_context: Understand that the accent color provides a secondary tint or sheen to certain lip styles.
evidence: Toggling the second color slider (Accent Color) while observing the lips
Character model remains static
Uncertain tone
Interrogative inflection
Scene: Lip Makeup Color sub-menu within character customization.
Expression: Squinting slightly at the screen and tilting her head as if trying to see a subtle change.
Quote: What is up with that accent color?

summary: Misleading UI grouping where 'Blemishes' (decals) are in the same menu block as standard makeup but do not share the same color-linking logic.
game_context: Understand that specific blemish decals (like the heart shapes) have locked or independent color profiles from the general cheek makeup sliders.
evidence: Scrolling through 'Blemish Color' while 'Cheek Makeup' (heart shapes) is selected
Hovering on color options that do not change the heart decals
Realization tone
Disappointed inflection
Scene: Cheek Makeup / Blemishes menu during character creation.
Expression: Slight frown followed by a dismissive head shake as she realizes the colors don't work the way she expected.
Quote: oh okay, I thought you could make like the hearts pink or something.
